text: Chapter 6: Africa Chapter 6: Africa explores the factors that will determine the success of the adoption and implementation of AI in Africa. This chapter begins by examining the concept of “responsible AI” and as it applies to AI developed and deployed in an African context. It then delves into the barriers it encountered including digital literacy gaps, lack of relevant local skills, and government restrictions. The chapter provides an in-depth assessment of the current state of African AI research and startup innovation, including notable initiatives and concerns. Finally, it concludes with a vision for a sustainable future of responsible AI in Africa and provides concrete recommendations to achieve it.
